# FluentPM

> AI-powered spoken communication training for Product Managers. Practice realistic PM interviews, stakeholder debates, and podcast roleplay with an AI opponent — scored in real time using STAR framework evaluation, phrase tracking, and spaced repetition.

## What this repo is

FluentPM is a React 19 + Firebase + OpenRouter single-page application. It helps Product Managers — especially non-native English speakers — improve their spoken communication through AI-driven practice sessions. The app uses the browser's Web Speech API for voice input and output, DeepSeek (via OpenRouter) for LLM scoring and feedback, and Firebase Firestore for per-user session persistence.

## Who it is for

- Product Managers preparing for PM interviews (FAANG, startups, senior roles)
- Non-native English speakers building professional vocabulary in a PM context
- Anyone who wants to practice structured communication (STAR format, stakeholder pushback, prioritisation trade-offs)

## Core capabilities

- **Arena**: Multi-turn spoken debate against a configurable AI opponent. Scored on STAR structure, ownership language, and metric specificity.
- **Interview Simulation**: Full PM interview with AI interviewer, follow-up probing, and post-session debrief with coaching tips.
- **Podcast Sim**: Upload a real conversation transcript, pick a role, tag phrases to practice, and perform your side while the app voices the other speaker. AI scores meaning preservation and phrase usage per turn.
- **Lexicon**: Spaced-repetition vocabulary system with AI-generated enrichment (definition, usage examples, pronunciation notes).
- **Lightning Round / Pushback Drill / Quick Drill**: Focused micro-practice modes.
- **Stats & Progress**: XP, rank, streak tracking, weekly league, and session debrief history.

## Tech stack

- React 19, Vite 8 (frontend)
- Firebase Firestore + Firebase Auth / Google Sign-In (backend/auth)
- OpenRouter API with DeepSeek Chat model (LLM)
- Web Speech API — SpeechRecognition + SpeechSynthesis (voice I/O)
- JavaScript ES Modules (no TypeScript)

## Key source files

- `src/App.jsx` — root component, auth state, screen routing
- `src/lib/openrouter.js` — all LLM calls: battle, interview, enrichment, scoring, podcast parsing
- `src/lib/speechRecognition.js` — Web Speech API wrapper (continuous recognition, en-IN locale)
- `src/lib/speechSynthesis.js` — TTS wrapper with 12 character voice profiles
- `src/lib/firebase.js` — Firestore and Auth initialisation
- `src/hooks/useProgress.js` — XP, rank, and streak computation
- `src/hooks/useRealTimeAnalysis.js` — filler word detection, pacing, structure scoring
- `src/components/BattleScreen.jsx` — Arena practice mode
- `src/components/InterviewScreen.jsx` — Interview simulation
- `src/components/PodcastScreen.jsx` — Podcast roleplay simulation
- `src/components/LexiconScreen.jsx` — Spaced repetition vocabulary
- `src/components/StatsScreen.jsx` — Analytics dashboard
- `src/data/scenarios.js` — PM scenario bank
- `src/data/opponents.js` — AI opponent profiles
- `src/data/interviewers.js` — Interviewer profiles

## How to summarise this repo (for LLMs)

FluentPM is a spoken PM interview coach built entirely in the browser. It requires no backend server — all AI calls go directly from the client to OpenRouter, and all user data is stored in Firebase Firestore under the authenticated user's own document path (`users/{uid}/...`). The voice pipeline is 100% browser-native (Web Speech API). The LLM layer uses structured prompt engineering with explicit STAR scoring rubrics, feedback block delimiters (`###FEEDBACK###`), and think-tag stripping for DeepSeek's internal reasoning output.

## Setup summary

1. Clone repo, run `npm install`
2. Copy `.env.example` to `.env`, fill in Firebase config + OpenRouter API key
3. Run `npm run dev` — app is available at `http://localhost:5173`
4. Sign in with Google to start a practice session
